summary: Impeccable soundboard captures a magic second set highlighted by Saint of Circumstance and an emotional Stella Blue, even though the first set feels forgettable.
review: The recording is a crystal‑clear soundboard tape with excellent drum mix and a guitar tone that really shines, though Weir can get lost in the balance at times. Musically the show delivers a standout second half – Saint of Circumstance, Foolish Heart, Lazy River Road and a soaring Stella Blue are repeatedly praised – while the first set is generally described as weak or “snoresville.” Overall fans rate the performance highly despite the uneven opening.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight strong musical moments; recording quality is repeatedly called excellent, giving a solid combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, immaculate soundboard capture with superb drum mix and highlighted Bobby guitar tone; occasional balance issue where Weir’s parts can get buried.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is widely described as forgettable or “snoresville,” but the second set is called magical, with tight transitions and high energy that lift the entire concert.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Aggressive, fast licks (like ’85) and emotional vocal/guitar work on Stella; trumpet‑like runs on Ramble on Rose praised.
band_member_comments.Bob: Gorgeous guitar tone highlighted throughout; Bobby’s playing is often singled out as a highlight of the tape.
band_member_comments.Keys: Trumpet‑style MIDI part during Ramble on Rose noted as a standout keyboard moment.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum mix praised as great in headphones, very trippy and “to die for” in the second set.
